claims.fieldOrBehavior: Iteration 2 (publish gate) full sweep: npm run build, npm run typecheck, npm run validate:source --, bash evals/run.sh static, bash evals/ci/antigaming-suite.sh (25 tests), and the 5 targeted integration evals (test_exemption_usage_review.sh, test_trust_reconcile_trailer_diagnostic.sh, test_veritas_governance_kit.sh, test_trust_reconcile.sh, test_trust_reconcile_negatives.sh) all exit 0, re-run fresh this iteration.

git diff origin/main -- src/tools/validate-source-tree.ts is 31 lines, NOT literally empty, but comment-only (zero non-comment lines differ; confirmed vs true merge-base 3dd7550 too) -- evidence-hygiene mismatch vs the assignment wording, not a functional defect. Independent bogus-path probe (built fresh, not reusing the eval file) confirms validate:source correctly fails naming the exact bogus path inside a kit skill dir, and passes again after removal. kit validate green with kit.json assets[] (schema additionalProperties:true confirmed). Transient finding: an unbounded self-recursive re-invocation existed mid-session in evals/static/test_validate_source_kit_asset_scope.sh (reproduced: 2-minute hard timeout, dozens of zombie processes, one ENOENT crash from a probe-file race) -- confirmed already fixed on disk to a single bounded --pre-flight-only re-invocation; re-run 3x clean post-fix, ~4.5s each, zero lingering processes.

Real DECLARED read-only run: review-exemptions.mjs against the committed delivery/DECLARED lists 2 entries; sha256 identical before/after (f9640ec5...); trust-reconcile.js exit code identical before/after (1/1). Trailer privacy negative independently reproduced via fresh scratch git repo + direct ESM import of findRuntimeSessionTrailers/logRuntimeSessionTrailers: positive case prints the exact ADR-0022-quoted line with the trailer KEY only (no VALUE/URL anywhere in output); negative case (no trailer) prints nothing and returns an empty array.

origin/main moved 2 commits ahead of this worktree (release 3.3.0 + #295 console-liveness-relay, unrelated to #303/#305/#304); diffing against the true merge-base 3dd7550 (== worktree HEAD) shows 7 modified + 7 new = 14 files, all explained (validator is comment-only-modified, contradicting the assignment one-liner but functionally inert; docs/context-map.md is a generated skill-index regeneration). bash evals/run.sh integration exits 1 with exactly 19 failures concentrated in 3 pre-existing, plan-unrelated files (test_goal_fit_hook.sh, test_goal_fit_escape_hatch.sh, test_session_resume_roundtrip.sh) -- none touch this plan's diff vs 3dd7550, all reproduced standalone. test_veritas_governance_kit.sh runs standalone (exit 0) but is not registered in evals/run.sh (pre-existing gap, predates this plan).

AC1 `review-lists-exemptions`: Given a `delivery/DECLARED` fixture (JSON array of `{scope, reason, approved_by, declared_at}`, mirroring the real 2-entry file), the skill's helper parses `git log --follow -- delivery/DECLARED` history within a review window and lists every entry with its scope, reason, approver, and age (now - `declared_at`)

AC2 `review-flags-stale`: Against a fixture with mixed fresh/stale entries (one `declared_at` inside a configurable age threshold, one outside), the review flags only the stale one as overdue for re-confirmation, using the exact age math (now-in-fixture minus `declared_at`)

AC3 `review-process-only-no-mutation`: Running the review does not modify `delivery/DECLARED` (byte-identical before/after) and does not change `scripts/ci/trust-reconcile.js`'s exit code when run against the same fixture tree before and after the review executes (the audit-vs-enforcement boundary is eval-locked, not just documented)

AC4 `skill-registered-documented`: The skill exists at `kits/veritas-governance/skills/exemption-usage-review/SKILL.md`, is documented in `kits/veritas-governance/docs/README.md`'s asset table (replacing the "Not in slice 1" deferral line), and `kit.json`'s container still validates (`flow kit validate`) with the skill present

AC5 `trailer-diagnostic-positive`: A PR/commit range containing a commit with a `Claude-Session:` (or `Codex-Session:`/`Opencode-Session:`/`Pi-Session:`) trailer produces the exact line `[trust-reconcile] identified: runtime-session trailer '<trailer>' on <ref>` in `trust-reconcile.js` stdout, for each distinct trailer found in range

AC6 `trailer-diagnostic-negative`: A commit range with no runtime-session trailer produces **no** such diagnostic line, and the reconciler's exit code is provably identical to a case with the trailer present (same canonical commands, same bundle/DECLARED state, differing only in trailer presence)
AC7 `trailer-diagnostic-checkout-depth-verified`: `.github/workflows/trust-reconcile.yml`'s checkout step is confirmed to already carry `fetch-depth: 0` (no `.yml` edit needed for #305's depth ask)

AC8 `declared-scope-forms-documented`: `delivery/README.md` contains a section documenting all four `DECLARED` scope forms (`ref:`, `commit:`/`commit:a..b`, `author:`, `branch-prefix:`) plus the compound space-separated-AND form and the `author:`+`branch-prefix:` combining requirement for identity exemptions, matching `matchesScopeCondition()`/`matchesScope()`'s actual implemented semantics (string equality/prefix only, no RegExp)

AC9 `no-fork-no-vendor`: The new skill/helper does not vendor or reimplement `trust-reconcile.js`'s scope-matching or DECLARED-parsing logic (`parseDeclaredMarker`, `matchesScope`) — it either shells out to read the same file format independently (read-only) or explicitly notes it intentionally does not need scope-matching (it lists ALL entries regardless of current-context scope, since the review's purpose is auditing every standing exemption, not evaluating which one currently applies)

AC10 `suites-green`: `npm run build && npm run eval:static && bash evals/run.sh integration` all exit 0 with the new eval files registered in `evals/run.sh`'s manually-curated list (integration evals are NOT auto-discovered — confirmed by inspection)

Scoped re-review of the trap-hygiene fix in evals/static/test_validate_source_kit_asset_scope.sh: verdict clean. Reviewer empirically reproduced the original SIGKILL failure (real kill -9 leaves stray probe; validate:source fails repo-wide) and confirmed heal_stale_probes() pre-flight removes it on the next invocation, restoring validate:source. Glob prefix test__scope_probe_ is unique repo-wide, scoped to evals/integration/, cannot match a legitimate file. --pre-flight-only shortcut is invoked only by the eval's own self-heal assertion; evals/run.sh invokes with zero args so CI always runs the full suite. Bash glob semantics verified under set -uo pipefail with nullglob/failglob off. One LOW (permissive arg handling fails in the safe direction: unknown args run the FULL eval), no CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM.
